_doc: Selective CLI access gating policy. Read by scripts/cli-gating/gate-cli-actions.sh (PreToolUse[Bash] hook). JSON not YAML — no PyYAML dependency. Design: assets/magi/20260521-0509-cli-gating-design/. Model: allowlist of READ verbs per CLI; everything else on a gated CLI is WRITE; env is 'dev' only when positively proven; prod-write/unknown-env-write -> BLOCK (exit 2); reads + proven-dev writes pass. Project .claude/cli-gating.json may tighten; loosening a prod gate needs allow_loosen:true.

gates._model: Read iff operation matches read_op_prefixes/read_ops_exact; everything else (create-/delete-/put-/update-/terminate-/run-/s3 cp|sync|mv|rm|mb|rb) is a WRITE and approval-gated per-command (any account). Conservative: an unlisted read just gets gated once; no write prefix is ever read. s3 cp/sync DOWNLOADS (dst is local) are gated too in v1 — known false-positive, approve once.
gates._sensitive_note: These read no state but return a live secret/credential -> approval-gated like a write. get-parameter* is gated only with --with-decryption (handled in code).
